items.question: A discrete random variable X has PMF p(0)=0.1, p(1)=0.4, p(2)=0.5. What is F(1) = P(X ≤ 1)?
For a continuous random variable X, which statement is always true?
options: P(X = x) = f(x) for all x
P(X = x) = 0 for any specific x
items.answer: P(X = x) = 0 for any specific x
items.explanation: Continuous RVs assign zero probability to any single point; probabilities come from integrating the PDF over intervals.

items.question: X ~ Uniform(0, 4). What is P(1 ≤ X ≤ 3)?
items.explanation: Uniform PDF is 1/4 on (0,4); interval length 2 gives probability 2 × (1/4) = 0.5.

items.question: A three-class softmax outputs [0.2, 0.5, 0.3]. What does 0.5 represent?
options: P(X = x) where x is a continuous value
items.explanation: Softmax produces a PMF over discrete class labels; 0.5 is the predicted probability mass at class index 1.
